prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans) gap of TEMPO, in eV.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (tempo.xyz) taken as-is; this was a single-point frontier-orbital analysis (no re-optimization).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge 0; spin multiplicity 2 (doublet, UKS).
Software: PySCF via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill (engine subcommand `frontier`).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 12 cycles; integrity status `ok`, trustworthy = true; no warnings reported by the engine.

Headline result
HOMO–LUMO gap: 3.8724203 eV
(HOMO = –6.3375439 eV, LUMO = –2.4651235 eV)

Reproducibility
CLI invocation (verbatim):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 2 --stdout path /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpon46itrw/live.json
Output JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpon46itrw/live.json (contains full result, orbitals, and integrity block).
A live PySCF log (.out) was streamed during the run; tail it for SCF details if further inspection is needed.
